Cause Greyed-out areas, folders, or menu options in Thunderbird are often caused by UI glitches, add-on conflicts, or synchronization issues. Areas are mostly frozen, greyed-out, or unusable. The symptoms can vary from minor to severe

Are you using chrome as browser?

Restarting in troubleshooting mode can help detect broken extensions or outdated add-ons that are cluttering our software.

First, repair the view layout. Once that is complete, you can switch to classic view.

Restarting in troubleshoot mode https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/troubleshoot-mode-thunderbird.

To use Restoring classic view. Open settings : click the hamburger menu>on the top-right tittle bar and select settings. Appearance tab :click on the appearance tab in the left side pane. Message list classic : in the right-side pane.find the message list section. Change view style : change the view style to table view. Enable classic view layout : click the hamburger menu again. Select>view>layout>classic view .

Tip Use a the latest version from us , thunderbird. https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/troubleshoot-mode-thunderbird

content:
I will assume you use Windows If you installed from Thunderbird.net, the profile is normally at 

c:\users\<yourid>\appdata\roaming\thunderbird

A simple way to locate it is to follow these steps;

  1. click help>troubleshootinginformation
  2. scroll down left side to 'profile folder' and click 'open folder'
  3. that will normally show the profile in the place described earlier.

If that is true, here are sample steps to backup and restore profile:

BACKUP

  1. ensure thunderbird is not running
  2. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  3. this should open windows file explorer in Appdata\roaming folder
  4. highlight the Thunderbird folder and copy to external media

To locate Thunderbird, if there is a desktop icon, rightclick and select 'properties.

RESTORE

  1. ensure thunderbird is installed and NOT running
  2. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  3. delete the thunderbird folder
  4. you should be in Appdata\roaming folder , copy&paste the exported Thunderbird folder there.
  5. start thunderbird and all should be there
creator: davidsk

content:
Go to TB menu > Settings > General

- scroll all the way down and click the 'Config editor' button on the right

- click 'Accept the risk and continue' if that appears

- search for mail.compose.attachment_reminder

Is it set to true?

Two entries below, is the mail.compose.attachment_reminder_keywords field populated? It should be this:

.doc,.pdf,.xls,.ppt,.rtf,.pps,attachment,attach,attached,attaching,enclosed,CV,cover letter

If both look right: does that also happen in Troubleshoot mode?
creator: siffemoz

content:
If you used the tools>export menu, passwords are not transferred for security reasons. If you use your OS file transfer facility, then passwords will be transferred, except where an email provider requires new password on new PC. Here are sample steps to transfer a profile:

  1. ensure thunderbird is NOT running on old machine
  2. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  3. this should open windows file explorer in Appdata\roaming folder
  4. highlight the Thunderbird folder and copy to external media
  5. ensure thunderbird is installed on second computer, started once and NOT running
  6. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  7. you should be in Appdata\roaming folder. DELETE the thunderbird folder there
  8. now , paste your copied thunderbird folder there. 
#   start thunderbird and all should be there
creator: davidsk

content:
1. As the account name will change, create a new account - but keep the old one
  2. Since this is a POP account, you will not lose any messages when the old account dies, as all messages are on PC
  3. the filers are in a file called msgfilterrules.dat. it is a simple text file, so you can edit it with notepad. whether they work or not will depend on careful editing to adjust text from old account name to new name. Often, a simple find/replace works. Anyway, with a minimum of work, this is doable. After tweaking it, COPY to the new account. To do that, click help>troubleshootinginformation, scroll to 'profile folder', click 'open folder', click the Mail folder and then the folder for old POP account, copy the msgfilterrules.dat file and then select the folder for the new POP account, locate its default msgfilterrules.dat file and overlay it.
  4. to 'clean up, I suggest copying the message folders from old account to Local Folders so that you don't lose them. A simple way for that is to click help>troubleshootinginformation, scroll again to 'profile folder' and click 'open folder' and, again, click the Mail folder and then the old POP folder. This time, look for message files, which will be in twos: e.g., inbox, inbox.msf, sent, sent.msf.. Ignore the msf files, but COPY the sister files and paste into the Mail\Local Folders file (rename if needed to prevent overlaying any existing files).
  5. once done, check that you salvaged all the messages and you can then delete the old account.

When done, be sure to backup everything. Here is a sample of backup and restore steps, if ever needed. BACKUP

  1. ensure thunderbird is not running
  2. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  3. this should open windows file explorer in Appdata\roaming folder
  4. highlight the Thunderbird folder and copy to external media

RESTORE

  1. ensure thunderbird is installed and NOT running
  2. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  3. delete the thunderbird folder
  4. you should be in Appdata\roaming folder , copy&paste the exported Thunderbird folder there.
  5. start thunderbird and all should be there
creator: davidsk

content: It may be your email provider. For example, I have a Gmail account and an Outlook account and I do NOT check the option to save a message in Sent folder, as Gmail and Outlook do that anyway. If I tick that box, two copies are sent. So, I am guessing that is your issue. If you provide the domain name of email provider, somone may be able to confirm that. And, if you use Gmail or Outlook, you now have an answer. :) Let me know.
